h1: Insurance Claims Data Extraction: Automate Claims Processing Workflow

hero.headline: Automate Insurance Claims Data Extraction
hero.subheadline: Extract structured data from adjuster reports, damage assessments, and policy documents into organized spreadsheets for faster claims processing

summary: Transform your insurance claims processing by automatically extracting critical data from adjuster reports, damage assessments, policy documents, and claim forms into structured Excel spreadsheets. This workflow reduces manual data entry time, minimizes errors, and creates consistent data formats for analysis and processing across your claims department.
whoItsFor: Insurance claims adjusters processing multiple reports daily
Claims departments managing high volumes of documentation
Insurance technology teams automating claims workflows
whenThisIsRelevant: Processing damage assessment reports from field adjusters
Converting scanned claim forms into digital databases
Extracting policy details from multiple insurance documents
supportedInputs: PDF adjuster reports and damage assessments
Scanned insurance claim forms and applications
Digital policy documents and coverage summaries
expectedOutputs: Structured spreadsheets with claim amounts, dates, and policy numbers
Organized data tables with damage descriptions and repair estimates
commonChallenges: Manual data entry from handwritten adjuster notes takes hours per claim
Inconsistent data formats across different adjusters and offices
Difficulty extracting specific amounts and dates from lengthy reports
Error-prone transcription of policy numbers and claim details
howItWorksSteps: Upload insurance documents (PDFs, scanned forms, or photos)
AI identifies and extracts key fields like claim numbers, amounts, and dates
Review extracted data and customize fields for your specific needs
Export organized data to Excel or CSV for your claims management system
whyPdfExcelAiFits: OCR technology reads both typed and reasonably clear handwritten text
Batch processing handles multiple claims documents simultaneously
Custom field selection adapts to different insurance form types
99%+ accuracy on clear digital documents reduces review time
limitations: Handwritten adjuster notes may require manual review for complex text
Heavily damaged or poor-quality scanned documents may have missing fields
Complex multi-page damage reports might need field customization for optimal results

faq.question: Can this extract data from handwritten adjuster reports?
faq.answer: Yes, but accuracy varies with handwriting clarity. Typed or printed text works best, while handwritten sections may need manual verification.

faq.question: What specific insurance data fields can be extracted?
faq.answer: Common fields include claim numbers, policy numbers, damage amounts, dates of loss, adjuster names, and property descriptions. Custom fields can be configured.

faq.question: How does batch processing work for multiple claims?
faq.answer: Upload multiple documents at once and the system processes them simultaneously, creating one spreadsheet row per document with consistent field mapping.

faq.question: Is the extracted claims data secure during processing?
faq.answer: Yes, files are encrypted during processing and automatically deleted after conversion to protect sensitive insurance information.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and deleted after processing for data security
